 {"id":"bd-1jt.4.3","title":"Add minisign verification to install.sh (optional but recommended)","description":"# Add Minisign Verification to install.sh (Optional but Recommended)

## Purpose
Verify downloaded binaries with minisign signatures to prevent tampering.

## Behavior
- If minisign available: verify by default
- If missing: warn, optionally install, or fail when --require-signatures
- Embed public key in installer (from dsr config)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] install.sh verifies .minisig when available
- [ ] Clear failure if signature invalid
- [ ] Non-interactive mode supports --require-signatures","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:37:11.776660104Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T22:13:27.576956969Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T22:13:27.576911403Z","close_reason":"Completed: Added _verify_minisign() function to install.sh template. Supports --require-signatures flag, embeds public key from config, downloads .minisig files, verifies with minisign tool.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.4.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.3.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:39:57.206972391Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.4.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.3.9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:39:57.459042194Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.4.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:37:11.776660104Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.4.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:39:57.697458578Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1jt.4.4","title":"Add installer cache/offline mode + gh release download fallback","description":"# Add Installer Cache/Offline Mode + gh release download Fallback

## Purpose
Improve reliability when network is flaky by caching release assets and allowing offline installs.

## Behavior
- Cache downloads in ~/.cache/dsr/installers/<tool>/<version>
- Support --cache-dir and --offline flags
- Prefer gh release download when gh is available (auth + retries)
- Fallback to curl when gh is missing

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Cached assets reused when available
- [ ] Offline mode fails fast with clear error if cache missing
- [ ] gh release download works for private repos","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:37:19.727170447Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T22:29:19.781146281Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T22:29:19.781128417Z","close_reason":"Implemented cache directory support (~/.cache/dsr/installers/<tool>/<version>), --cache-dir and --offline flags, auto-caching on download, and gh release download fallback for private repos. Added test_install_gen.sh with 9 passing tests.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.4.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.1.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:39:02.979791158Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.4.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:37:19.727170447Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.4.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:40:04.030679358Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1jt.4.5","title":"Provide PowerShell installer wrapper for native Windows","description":"# Provide PowerShell Installer Wrapper for Native Windows

## Purpose
Offer a first-class Windows install path (install.ps1) that mirrors install.sh behavior for users not using WSL.

## Behavior
- Detect OS/arch, download matching .exe
- Verify SHA256 (and minisign if available)
- Install to user-writable PATH (e.g., %LOCALAPPDATA%\Programs\dsr\bin)
- Support `-Yes`, `-Verbose`, `-Version`, `-Prefix`, `-CacheDir`, `-Offline`
- Respect non-interactive mode (`$env:CI=1` or `-Yes`)

## Logging
- Human-readable logs to stderr
- Optional JSON output for automation

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] install.ps1 parity with install.sh (flags, logs)
- [ ] Works on Windows 10/11 without admin
- [ ] Signature verification supported when minisign present
- [ ] Documented usage in README","status":"in_progress","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:37:25.838673495Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-31T01:05:11.612389489Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.4.5","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.1.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:39:03.227926195Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.4.5","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:37:25.838673495Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.4.5","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:40:08.359019185Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.4.5","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.4.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:40:08.618811604Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.4.5","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.4.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:40:08.857268243Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-1jt.5","title":"Theme 5: Testing & Validation","description":"# Testing & Validation

## Purpose
Ensure all components of the fallback release system work correctly through comprehensive unit, integration, and E2E tests with detailed logging.

## Testing Strategy

### 1. Unit Tests
- Platform detection
- Release freshness calculation
- Checksum/signature verification
- Toolchain detection and guardrails

### 2. Integration Tests
- Check → Build → Release pipeline (mocked GH/SSH)
- Repos registry management
- Status/report + prune dry-run

### 3. Contract Tests
- JSON schema validation for **every** `--json` output
- Stream separation: stderr empty on success

### 4. E2E Tests
- Docker containers for Linux installers
- Act-based fallback pipeline (mocked release)
- Package manager update workflow (Homebrew/Scoop) in dry-run

## Test Framework
Use bats-core for Bash tests.

## Logging Requirements
All tests must capture and print logs on failure (shared harness).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Unit + integration tests run <5 minutes
- [ ] E2E tests run <30 minutes
- [ ] JSON schema + stream separation validated
- [ ] Detailed logs available for failures","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:10:44.485219623Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T21:34:57.211188096Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.5","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:10:44.485219623Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-1jt.5.1","title":"Write unit tests for platform detection and freshness checking","description":"# Unit Tests: Platform Detection & Freshness Checking

## Test File
tests/unit/test_core_functions.bats

## Platform Detection Tests

### Test Cases
\`\`\`bash
@test "detect Linux x86_64" {
  mock_uname "Linux" "x86_64"
  run detect_platform
  assert_equal "\$PLATFORM" "linux-amd64"
}

@test "detect Linux ARM64" {
  mock_uname "Linux" "aarch64"
  run detect_platform
  assert_equal "\$PLATFORM" "linux-arm64"
}

@test "detect macOS ARM64 (Apple Silicon)" {
  mock_uname "Darwin" "arm64"
  run detect_platform
  assert_equal "\$PLATFORM" "darwin-arm64"
}

@test "detect macOS x86_64 (Intel)" {
  mock_uname "Darwin" "x86_64"
  run detect_platform
  assert_equal "\$PLATFORM" "darwin-amd64"
}

@test "detect Windows via MINGW" {
  mock_uname "MINGW64_NT-10.0" "x86_64"
  run detect_platform
  assert_equal "\$PLATFORM" "windows-amd64"
}

@test "unsupported OS fails gracefully" {
  mock_uname "FreeBSD" "amd64"
  run detect_platform
  assert_failure
  assert_output --partial "Unsupported OS"
}

@test "unsupported arch fails gracefully" {
  mock_uname "Linux" "riscv64"
  run detect_platform
  assert_failure
  assert_output --partial "Unsupported architecture"
}
\`\`\`

## Freshness Check Tests

### Test Cases
\`\`\`bash
@test "release 0 commits behind is fresh" {
  mock_gh_compare 0
  run check_freshness "v1.0.0"
  assert_success
}

@test "release 10 commits behind is fresh (boundary)" {
  mock_gh_compare 10
  run check_freshness "v1.0.0"
  assert_success
}

@test "release 11 commits behind is stale (boundary)" {
  mock_gh_compare 11
  run check_freshness "v1.0.0"
  assert_failure
  assert_output --partial "commits behind"
}

@test "release 100 commits behind is very stale" {
  mock_gh_compare 100
  run check_freshness "v1.0.0"
  assert_failure
  assert_output --partial "100 commits behind"
}

@test "freshness check handles API error" {
  mock_gh_error "rate limit exceeded"
  run check_freshness "v1.0.0"
  # Should warn but not fail hard
  assert_success
  assert_output --partial "Could not check freshness"
}

@test "freshness check without gh CLI uses curl fallback" {
  unset_command "gh"
  mock_curl_compare 5
  run check_freshness "v1.0.0"
  assert_success
}
\`\`\`

## Mock Helpers
\`\`\`bash
# tests/helpers/mocks.bash

mock_uname() {
  local os="\$1" arch="\$2"
  function uname() {
    case "\$1" in
      -s) echo "\$os" ;;
      -m) echo "\$arch" ;;
    esac
  }
  export -f uname
}

mock_gh_compare() {
  local ahead_by="\$1"
  function gh() {
    if [[ "\$*" == *"compare"* ]]; then
      echo "{\"ahead_by\": \$ahead_by}"
    fi
  }
  export -f gh
}

mock_gh_error() {
  local error="\$1"
  function gh() {
    echo "gh: \$error" >&2
    return 1
  }
  export -f gh
}
\`\`\`

## Logging Output
Each test logs:
- Input parameters
- Mock configuration
- Function output
- Assertions checked

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All platform combinations tested
- [ ] Boundary conditions for freshness tested
- [ ] Error cases handled
- [ ] Mock helpers reusable
- [ ] Tests run in <10 seconds","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:11:32.707115669Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:34.630160141Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:34.630141697Z","close_reason":"Unit tests for platform detection and freshness checking implemented in tests/unit/test_core_functions.bats - all 29 tests pass","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.5.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:15:53.451303881Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.5.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:11:32.707115669Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.5.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.5.9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T16:53:11.550295352Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

- [ ] Mocked API adapter tests with cache hits/misses

- [ ] Safe delete refuses non-dsr paths
- [ ] Logging layout matches XDG spec","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:37:37.282238775Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T21:56:00.040915982Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T21:56:00.040772292Z","close_reason":"Added XDG layout tests to test_logging.sh (date-based dirs, latest symlink).  {"id":"bd-1jt.5.16","title":"Write tests for signing module (key mgmt + batch signing)","description":"## Purpose
Test the `src/signing.sh` module which provides minisign key management and artifact signing.

## Unit Tests (tests/unit/test_signing.bats)

### Key Management
- signing_init creates keypair with password protection
- signing_check fails when private/public key missing
- signing_fix_permissions sets correct mode (chmod 600)

### Signing Operations
- signing_sign creates .minisig file
- signing_verify validates signatures
- signing_sign_batch signs multiple files

### Error Handling
- signing_sign fails gracefully when minisign missing
- signing_verify fails on tampered file

## Logging Requirements
- All test failures must output full signing command and error
- Key paths logged for debugging permission issues
- Timing logged for batch operations

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Unit tests for all signing_* functions
- [ ] Permission validation tests
- [ ] Batch signing tests with multiple files
- [ ] Error handling tests (missing minisign, bad perms, verification failures)
- [ ] Tests run in < 10 seconds
- [ ] Logs captured on failure","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T16:51:42.972843202Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T18:35:15.783971352Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T18:35:15.783461381Z","close_reason":"Added signing module tests (minisign stub)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.5.16","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.3.9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T16:52:06.952317915Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.5.16","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T16:51:42.972843202Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.5.16","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.5.9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T16:52:10.290497048Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-1jt.5.17","title":"Write tests for supply chain security (SLSA, SBOM, quality gates)","description":"# Tests for Supply Chain Security Features

## Purpose
Test SLSA provenance attestation, SBOM generation, and pre-release quality gates.

## Test Coverage

### SLSA Provenance Tests (tests/unit/test_slsa.bats)
```bash
@test "slsa_generate creates valid in-toto statement" {
  local artifact="$BATS_TEST_TMPDIR/test-binary"
  echo "binary" > "$artifact"
  
  run slsa_generate "$artifact" --builder "dsr/v1"
  assert_success
  
  # Verify JSON structure
  local stmt
  stmt="$(cat "${artifact}.intoto.jsonl")"
  assert_equal "$(echo "$stmt" | jq -r '._type')" "https://in-toto.io/Statement/v1"
  assert_equal "$(echo "$stmt" | jq -r '.predicateType')" "https://slsa.dev/provenance/v1"
}

@test "slsa_generate includes correct subject digest" {
  local artifact="$BATS_TEST_TMPDIR/test-binary"
  echo "binary content" > "$artifact"
  local expected_sha256
  expected_sha256="$(sha256sum "$artifact" | cut -d' ' -f1)"
  
  run slsa_generate "$artifact"
  assert_success
  
  local actual_sha256
  actual_sha256="$(cat "${artifact}.intoto.jsonl" | jq -r '.subject[0].digest.sha256')"
  assert_equal "$actual_sha256" "$expected_sha256"
}

@test "slsa_verify validates attestation" {
  # Create artifact + valid attestation
  local artifact="$BATS_TEST_TMPDIR/test-binary"
  echo "binary" > "$artifact"
  slsa_generate "$artifact"
  
  run slsa_verify "$artifact"
  assert_success
}

@test "slsa_verify fails on tampered artifact" {
  local artifact="$BATS_TEST_TMPDIR/test-binary"
  echo "binary" > "$artifact"
  slsa_generate "$artifact"
  
  # Tamper with artifact
  echo "modified" > "$artifact"
  
  run slsa_verify "$artifact"
  assert_failure
  assert_output --partial "digest mismatch"
}
```

### SBOM Tests (tests/unit/test_sbom.bats)
```bash
@test "sbom_generate creates SPDX JSON" {
  local project="$BATS_TEST_TMPDIR/project"
  mkdir -p "$project"
  echo '[package]
name = "test-crate"
version = "1.0.0"' > "$project/Cargo.toml"
  
  run sbom_generate "$project" --format spdx
  assert_success
  assert [ -f "$project/sbom.spdx.json" ]
  
  # Verify SPDX structure
  assert_equal "$(jq -r '.spdxVersion' "$project/sbom.spdx.json")" "SPDX-2.3"
}

@test "sbom_generate creates CycloneDX JSON" {
  local project="$BATS_TEST_TMPDIR/project"
  mkdir -p "$project"
  echo '{"name": "test-pkg", "version": "1.0.0"}' > "$project/package.json"
  
  run sbom_generate "$project" --format cyclonedx
  assert_success
  assert [ -f "$project/sbom.cdx.json" ]
}

@test "sbom_generate handles missing deps gracefully" {
  local project="$BATS_TEST_TMPDIR/empty"
  mkdir -p "$project"
  
  run sbom_generate "$project"
  assert_failure
  assert_output --partial "No package manifest found"
}
```

### Quality Gates Tests (tests/unit/test_quality_gates.bats)
```bash
@test "quality_gates runs configured checks" {
  # Mock check commands
  function cargo() { echo "Check passed"; return 0; }
  function go() { echo "Go vet passed"; return 0; }
  export -f cargo go
  
  run quality_gates "ntm" --config "$FIXTURES/repos-with-checks.yaml"
  assert_success
  assert_output --partial "All checks passed"
}

@test "quality_gates fails on blocking check failure" {
  function cargo() { echo "Error: unused import"; return 1; }
  export -f cargo
  
  run quality_gates "test-tool" --config "$FIXTURES/repos-with-checks.yaml"
  assert_failure
  assert_output --partial "Blocking check failed"
}

@test "quality_gates continues on allow_fail checks" {
  # lint fails but is in allow_fail
  function lint_tool() { return 1; }
  function test_tool() { return 0; }
  export -f lint_tool test_tool
  
  run quality_gates "test-tool" --config "$FIXTURES/repos-allow-fail.yaml"
  assert_success
  assert_output --partial "Non-blocking check failed: lint"
}

@test "quality_gates skipped with --skip-checks" {
  run quality_gates "ntm" --skip-checks
  assert_success
  assert_output --partial "Quality gates skipped"
}

@test "quality_gates produces JSON report" {
  run quality_gates "ntm" --json
  assert_success
  
  # Verify JSON structure
  echo "$output" | jq -e '.checks | length > 0'
  echo "$output" | jq -e '.passed == true'
}
```

### Integration Tests
```bash
@test "release includes SLSA + SBOM when enabled" {
  # Setup mock release
  run dsr release test-tool v1.0.0 --dry-run --with-slsa --with-sbom
  assert_success
  assert_output --partial "Would upload: test-tool.intoto.jsonl"
  assert_output --partial "Would upload: sbom.spdx.json"
}
```

## Fixtures
- tests/fixtures/repos-with-checks.yaml
- tests/fixtures/repos-allow-fail.yaml
- tests/fixtures/sample-cargo-project/
- tests/fixtures/sample-go-project/

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] SLSA generation/verification tested
- [ ] SBOM generation for Rust/Go/Node tested
- [ ] Quality gates pass/fail/skip tested
- [ ] JSON reports validated
- [ ] Logs captured on failure","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T17:16:54.479330276Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T17:17:07.200941306Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.5.17","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.3.11","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T17:17:05.094073940Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.5.17","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.3.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T17:17:03.131169129Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.5.17","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.3.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T17:17:01.029188812Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.5.17","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T17:16:54.479330276Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.5.17","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.5.9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T17:17:07.200845515Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-1jt.5.18","title":"Schema + stream separation tests (JSON purity)","description":"# Schema + Stream Separation Tests (JSON Purity)

## Purpose
Guarantee that every `dsr --json` output is schema-valid and that stderr contains no human noise unless an error occurs.

## Test Files
- `tests/integration/test_json_envelope.bats`
- `tests/fixtures/` (command outputs + schema fixtures)

## Coverage
- `dsr check/watch/build/release/fallback/repos/status/report/prune/config/doctor --json`
- Validate against `schemas/envelope.json` + command-specific schemas
- Assert **stderr empty** in success mode

## Tooling
- Prefer `ajv` for full schema validation
- Fallback to `jq` structural checks when ajv missing

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Each command’s JSON conforms to schema
- [ ] No stderr output in successful `--json` runs
- [ ] Failing commands include error envelope + non-zero exit
- [ ] Tests log full stdout/stderr on failure","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T17:37:17.149755251Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T22:37:22.139094726Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T22:37:22.138860886Z","close_reason":"Created test_json_purity.sh with 16 tests covering: JSON validity per command (doctor, status, config, repos, check), envelope schema compliance (required fields, valid status/exit_code, correct tool), stream separation (stderr handling), command field validation, and error response structure.  {"id":"bd-1jt.5.2","title":"Write E2E tests for installer scripts using Docker","description":"# E2E Tests for Installer Scripts

## Purpose
Validate that generated install.sh scripts work correctly in real environments using Docker containers.

## Test File
`tests/e2e/test_installers.bats`

## Test Environments
- ubuntu:22.04 (Linux x64)
- debian:bookworm (alt Linux)
- arm64v8/ubuntu:22.04 (Linux ARM64, QEMU)

## Test Cases
- Basic install (binary download)
- Source build path (`--source`)
- Stale release detection
- Checksum verification
- Minisign verification (when available)
- Cache/offline mode (if enabled)
- Custom install prefix

## Logging
Each E2E test captures:
- Full Docker output
- Installer verbose logs
- Timing info

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Tests pass on Ubuntu x64
- [ ] Source build tests work
- [ ] Signature + checksum verification tested
- [ ] Cache/offline mode tested
- [ ] Tests complete in 30 minutes or less","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:13:18.607161544Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T23:38:51.122576639Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T23:38:51.122545961Z","close_reason":"E2E Docker tests created in tests/e2e/test_installers_docker.sh. 12 tests (11 pass, 1 skip for ARM64).
